rationale: Keep: the citing paper explicitly frames a gap in "sparse B2B evidence on outcomes" and aims to link satisfaction to financial performance, while the cited 2004 paper is a B2B customer-level model that links effort/attribute performance/satisfaction to share of wallet and customer margin. Given this strong topical and construct overlap (B2B satisfaction  financial outcomes) and an exact DOI match, this does not look like an obvious incidental mention.
